arts.ttl: Mesut Ozil tells former team-mate that Arsenal move was 'serious mistake'
arts.sum: Mesut Ozil joined Arsenal from Real Madrid for £42.5million in 2013, but his former team-mate Sami Khedira has revealed he now views the transfer as a "serious mistake"

arts.ttl: ‘Diversity is our weapon’: inside Monaco’s eclectic football approach
arts.sum: Arsenal’s Wednesday opponents reveal how an integrated policy of buy young, pay well, sell high works for them and their sole sister club, Cercle Brugge Monaco’s training ground, carved into a hillside above the principality, overlooks the Côte d’Azur. It is the sort of glamorous setting expected for a football team in one of the world’s richest corners. Fifteen nationalities file out of the changing rooms and on to the pitch to be coached by an Austrian, driving home the club’s eclectic nature. They are the senior partner in a multi‑club operation that involves one other team: Cercle Brugge, who were bought in 2017. Whereas others stockpile clubs, two is the height of the ambition here, the thinking being that it provides agility but ensures focus is not diverted. Clubs live and die by recruitment and investing in another team allows more access to talent and increased scope to develop it. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal's expected XI vs Monaco as Mikel Arteta recalls forgotten man amid injury crisis
arts.sum: Arsenal are gunning to make it back-to-back victories in the Champions League and looking to respond to Sunday's 1-1 draw away to Fulham which dented their domestic title hopes

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Arsenal ready to rival Newcastle & Aston Villa for €40m attacker transfer
